steps.title: Identify the center and radius of circle A
steps.content: The standard form of a circle is $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$. Circle A has equation $$(x-7)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$, so the center is $$(7,-3)$$ and the radius is $$1$$.

steps.title: Apply the translation right 4 units
steps.content: Translating right by 4 adds 4 to the x-coordinate of the center. New center: $$(7+4,-3)=(11,-3)$$. The radius stays $$1$$. Circle B's equation is $$(x-11)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$(x-7)^2+(y+7)^2=1$': Center would be (7,-7). The x did not shift and y changed incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(x-3)^2+(y+3)^2=1$': Center would be (3,-3). That shifts left 4, not right. ✗
  • Choice C — '$(x-11)^2+(y+3)^2=1$': Center is (11,-3), exactly 4 right of (7,-3). ✓
  • Choice D — '$(x-7)^2+(y-1)^2=1$': Center would be (7,1). The x did not shift and y changed incorrectly. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
